heading: Licenses: military reservations; erection and use of buildings; Young Men’s Christian Association
label: 7778 Licenses: military reservations; erection and use of buildings; Young Men’s Christian Association
text: Under such conditions as he may prescribe, the Secretary of the Army may issue a revocable license to the International Committee of Young Men’s Christian Associations of North America to erect and maintain on military reservations, inside the United States and the Commonwealths and possessions, buildings needed by that organization for the promotion of the social, physical, intellectual, and moral welfare of the members of the Army on those reservations.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)477810:1346., .May 31, 1902, ch. 94332 Stat. 282The words “may issue” are substituted for the words “Authority is given to * * * in his discretion, to grant permission”. The words “Under such conditions as he may prescribe” are substituted for the words “under such regulations as the Secretary of the Army may impose”. The words “members of the Army” are substituted for the word “garrisons”. The words “the Territories, Commonwealths, and possessions” are substituted for the words “or its island possessions” for clarity.

Codification, , , which directed the substitution of “Commonwealths or possessions” for “Territories, Commonwealths, or possessions” in this section, but which could not be executed because “Territories, Commonwealths, or possessions” did not appear in text, was amended, effective as if included in as enacted, so as to not apply to this section by , , .Pub.
